title: What is an LVAD?
subtitle: Understanding Your Heart Device
description: Learn the basics of Left Ventricular Assist Device therapy, how it works, and what to expect.

sections.title: Introduction to LVAD
sections.content: A Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD) is a mechanical pump surgically implanted to help your heart pump blood throughout your body. It supports — but does not replace — your heart.
sections.imageAlt: Diagram showing LVAD placement in the chest

sections.title: How Blood Flows
sections.content: Blood flows from the left ventricle into the LVAD pump, which then pushes it into the aorta and out to your body. The pump runs continuously, powered by batteries you carry with you.

sections.title: Types of LVAD Devices
items: HeartMate 3 — Current generation, centrifugal flow, with artificial pulse
HeartWare HVAD — No longer implanted but still in use in existing patients
Future devices — Clinical trials ongoing for next-generation pumps

sections.title: Life Expectations
sections.content: Most patients report improved energy and quality of life within weeks of LVAD implant. However, you will always carry external equipment including a controller and batteries.

sections.title: Key Equipment Components
items: The implanted pump — inside your chest
Driveline — cable connecting pump to external controller
System controller — manages and monitors the pump
Power module — for charging and AC power use

quiz.question: What does LVAD stand for?
quiz.explanation: LVAD stands for Left Ventricular Assist Device — a mechanical pump that helps the heart's left ventricle pump blood.

quiz.question: Where does blood enter the LVAD?
quiz.explanation: Blood enters the LVAD from the left ventricle, and is then pumped out into the aorta.

quiz.question: The LVAD completely replaces the heart's function.
quiz.explanation: False. The LVAD assists or supports the heart — it does not replace it. Your heart continues to work alongside the device.

quiz.question: Which of the following is the current generation LVAD device?
quiz.explanation: The HeartMate 3 is the current standard centrifugal flow LVAD with an artificial pulse feature.

quiz.question: What connects the implanted pump to the external controller?
quiz.explanation: The driveline is the cable that exits your body through a small hole in the skin and connects the implanted pump to the external system controller.
